categories.Title: PMS before your period starts
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries and uterus. Symptoms occur before menstruation."

categories.Title: Heavy periods with clotting
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries and uterus. Heavy menstrual bleeding with clotting."

categories.Title: Periods becoming more normal
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries and uterus. Periods becoming more regular after previously being heavy."

categories.Title: Beginning to skip periods
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries and uterus. Early perimenopause."

categories.Title: No periods
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries and uterus. Menopause or no menstrual periods."

categories.Title: Ovaries - no uterus (ages 20-35)
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries but no uterus."

categories.Title: Ovaries - no uterus (ages 35-45)
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries but no uterus."

categories.Title: Ovaries - no uterus (ages 45+)
categories.Description: "For those with ovaries but no uterus."

categories.Title: No ovaries - All ages
categories.Description: "For those with no ovaries; regardless of uterus status."

symptoms.Notes: Display wording normalized from 'Auto immune disorders'.

symptoms.OriginalPDFName: Lowered immune function / Lowered immune response
symptoms.Notes: Merged with PDF wording 'Lowered immune function' from page 1.
Appears in PDF under Female Health and Sexual; assigned to Sexual as the master section.

resources.Title: You're Not Losing Your Mind; You're Losing Your Hormones!
How to Customize Your Hormone Therapy

recommendations.Name: Estradiol
recommendations.AssessmentSummary: Your responses suggest that low estrogen may be contributing to your symptoms.
recommendations.ProductSummary: Supports healthy estrogen balance during menopause.

recommendations.Name: Progesterone
recommendations.AssessmentSummary: Your responses suggest that low progesterone may be contributing to your symptoms.
recommendations.ProductSummary: Supports healthy progesterone balance and hormonal wellness.

recommendations.Name: Melatonin
recommendations.AssessmentSummary: Your responses suggest that sleep-related symptoms may be contributing to your overall hormone balance picture.
recommendations.ProductSummary: Supports restful sleep and nighttime recovery.

recommendations.Name: Cortisol Support
recommendations.AssessmentSummary: Your responses suggest that cortisol imbalance may be contributing to your symptoms.
recommendations.ProductSummary: Supports healthy cortisol rhythm and stress response.
